proofs.proof_tex:
After the approved corrections in the escalation log, the rate-characterization node no longer asserts a two-sided proved bracket; it records the proved converse and the fact that feasible weak-overlap achievability is still open on the frozen core. We verify exactly that corrected content.

First, the lower bound is already established by thm:minimax-lower. Under the stated policy-richness condition, there exists a constant \(c>0\), independent of \(n\), such that for all large \(n\),
\[
M_n(\alpha,\gamma)\ge c n^{-r_\star(\alpha,\gamma)}.
\]
By def:exponents,
\[
r_\star(\alpha,\gamma)=\frac{1+\alpha}{2+\alpha+\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}}.
\]
This proves the converse half of the corrected theorem.

Second, we explain why no feasible upper exponent is part of the proved characterization. The corrected definition def:feasible-rate expressly reserves \(hbar_n\) and \(qbar_n\) as oracle design scalars and states that no feasible exponent \(r_{\mathrm{feas}}\) is fixed by the present primitives. This is not editorial; it matches the exact algebra of the feasible score drift. By lem:clip-bias, for any plug-in nuisance triple \(\bar\eta=(\bar\mu_0,\bar\mu_1,\bar e)\),
\[
E_P[\Gamma_q(O;\bar\eta)\mid X=x]-\tau_P(x)
=(\bar e_q(x)-e_P(x))\left(\frac{\Delta_1(x)}{\bar e_q(x)}+\frac{\Delta_0(x)}{1-\bar e_q(x)}\right),
\]
where \(\bar e_q(x)=\min\{1-q,\max\{q,\bar e(x)\}\}\) and \(\Delta_a=\bar\mu_a-\mu_a\). Thus the feasible drift is a product of a propensity error factor and a regression-error factor. It is not an additive clipping term supported only on \(\{p_P\le q\}\).

The stalled route for oeq:feasible-upper requires a bound of the schematic form
\[
\|\bar e_q-e_P\|_{L_2(P_X)} \lesssim r_{e,n}+ q\,P\{p_P(X)\le q\}^{1/2},
\]
which would turn the drift into a balanceable quantity \(r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}/q + r_{\mu,n} q^{1/(2\gamma)}\). But this localization is not supplied by the frozen assumptions. Indeed, the clipping displacement \(\bar e_q-\bar e\) is supported on the event \(\{\bar e<q\}\cup\{\bar e>1-q\}\), which is determined by the estimated propensity \(\bar e\), whereas ass:marginal-overlap controls only the true-law event \(\{p_P\le q\}\). Under ass:nuisance-rate one has only a global \(L_2(P_X)\) bound on \(\bar e-e_P\); no sup-norm or classification-type control is available that would force \(\{\bar e<q\}\subseteq \{p_P\le cq\}\cup G_n\) with a quantitatively negligible remainder \(G_n\). Therefore the proposed clip-displacement localization, and hence the advertised closed-form feasible exponent, do not follow from the declared dependencies.

Because the upper-rate node oeq:feasible-upper remains genuinely open, the corrected rate-characterization theorem consists precisely of the proved converse bound together with the statement that feasible weak-overlap attainability is unresolved on the present core. That is exactly what has been shown.

open_obligations.what_is_open: Derive a valid closed-form welfare-regret upper bound for the genuinely feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm under the frozen assumptions, or else prove that no such bound of the advertised balancing form can hold on the present class.
open_obligations.obstruction: The route encoded in the target statement uses the exact drift identity from lem:clip-bias, but then makes a localization step that is not justified by the frozen primitives. The drift factor is (bar e_q-e_P)(Delta_1/bar e_q + Delta_0/(1-bar e_q)). To turn this into a balanceable clipped-region term one must control the clipping displacement bar e_q-bar e by the true rare-overlap set {p_P<=q}. However bar e_q-bar e is supported on {bar e<q} union {bar e>1-q}, an event defined by the estimated propensity, while ass:marginal-overlap controls only the true-law event {p_P<=q}. Assumption ass:nuisance-rate gives only global L2 bounds on ehat-e_P and muhat-mu, not the q-localized sup-norm or margin-type control needed to compare these events. Consequently the claimed bound B(q) <= r_mu_n r_e_n / q + r_mu_n q^{1/(2 gamma)} is not derivable from the frozen dependencies. The gamma=0 endpoint is also not closed by ass:marginal-overlap, because its stated convention gives no vanishing q-tail to optimize against.
open_obligations.attempted: Route 1: apply Cauchy-Schwarz directly to the exact drift identity. This yields |E[(pi-pi_star)b_q]| <= q^{-1} ||bar e_q-e_P||_2 (||Delta_0||_2+||Delta_1||_2), but leaves ||bar e_q-e_P||_2 nonlocal. Route 2: split ||bar e_q-e_P||_2 <= ||bar e-e_P||_2 + ||bar e_q-bar e||_2 and try to bound the second term by q P{p_P<=q}^{1/2}. This fails because the support of bar e_q-bar e is determined by bar e, not by p_P. Route 3: combine thm:margin-localization with ass:vc-localized-envelope to control the oracle empirical-process term. This recovers the usual localized stochastic scale n^{-1/2} q^{-1/2} rho^{alpha/(2+2 alpha)} polylog(n), but it does not resolve the feasible drift. Route 4: use only the global L2 bound ||bar e_q-e_P||_2 <= ||bar e-e_P||_2 + 1, which is too crude to yield any meaningful optimization in q or any frontier-advancing rate theorem.
open_obligations.partial_result: Two pieces are proved. First, thm:minimax-lower gives the sharp converse benchmark M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}. Second, the achievability route is reduced exactly to the product-form clipped-score drift from lem:clip-bias together with the oracle localized empirical-process term supplied by thm:margin-localization and ass:vc-localized-envelope. Concretely, for a regret radius rho, the stochastic offset term has oracle scale A_n(q,rho) of order n^{-1/2} q^{-1/2} rho^{alpha/(2+2 alpha)} polylog(n), while the feasible obstruction is entirely in controlling ||bar e_q-e_P||_2 inside q^{-1} ||bar e_q-e_P||_2 (||Delta_0||_2+||Delta_1||_2). Any next-round resolution must therefore add a new argument that localizes the estimated clipping event to the true weak-overlap region, or else show by counterexample that such a localization is impossible on the current core.

open_obligations.what_is_open: Determine whether any genuinely feasible estimator, and in particular the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm, can attain the converse exponent r_star(alpha,gamma) under weak overlap, or whether feasible weak-arm nuisance learning necessarily forces a strictly slower welfare-regret rate.
open_obligations.obstruction: This tightness question cannot be settled before oeq:feasible-upper is closed. The present frozen core proves the lower benchmark r_star through thm:minimax-lower, but it proves no feasible upper rate and no feasible lower obstruction beyond the minimax converse. Without either an achievability theorem at exponent r_star or a separate impossibility theorem showing every feasible method is slower, the comparison required for tightness is mathematically undetermined.
open_obligations.attempted: I reduced the question to the precise obstruction in oeq:feasible-upper. Every attempted resolution of tightness first requires a feasible upper theorem, because the only proved rate statement presently available is the converse. No additional argument from the current dependencies yields a feasible-achievability exponent or a feasible-only lower bound.
open_obligations.partial_result: The converse exponent r_star(alpha,gamma) is fully identified and proved sharp as a minimax lower benchmark on the full class by thm:minimax-lower. The exact source of the feasible difficulty is also identified: it is the nonlocal product drift in lem:clip-bias, not the oracle empirical-process term. Thus the residual open problem is sharply formulated: either devise a feasible estimator and proof that overcome that drift at rate n^{-r_star}, or prove that this is impossible and a slower feasible frontier prevails.
